evals.name: full-sweep

assertions.trap: Hardcoded strings: 'Settings', 'Display Name', 'Email', 'Bio', 'Password', 'Confirm Password', 'Save changes', 'Last saved:', title tag
assertions.description: ZERO hardcoded user-visible text. Every string the user sees goes through t(). No exceptions.

assertions.trap: marginLeft, paddingRight, textAlign: 'left', borderLeft used instead of CSS logical properties
assertions.description: Replace directional CSS (margin-left, padding-right, text-align: left, border-left) with logical equivalents (margin-inline-start, padding-inline-end, text-align: start, border-inline-start).

assertions.trap: Config uses only navigator.language with no user preference or URL-based detection priority
assertions.description: Locale detection priority chain: user preference first, then URL, subdomain, Accept-Language header, navigator.language, then default fallback.

assertions.trap: Translation file is JSON instead of TypeScript exported as const
assertions.description: Single TypeScript file per locale exported as const for type inference, no JSON parsing.

assertions.trap: Keys like 'Welcome to the app' and 'Sign in' lack domain prefix; validation/errors are nested objects
assertions.description: Flat keys with domain prefix (domain.key). Never nest more than 2 levels.

assertions.trap: 'Welcome to the app' and 'Sign in' use English content as keys instead of element-descriptive keys
assertions.description: Translation key naming: keys describe the element, not the content. Keys must survive renaming of the English string.

assertions.trap: 'diffs.header.actions.delete.confirm' is nested 5 levels deep
assertions.description: Never nest more than 2 levels: 'diffs.files' is fine, 'diffs.header.actions.delete.confirm' is not.

assertions.trap: <Trans> used for simple text that doesn't require JSX interpolation
assertions.description: Never use <Trans> component unless interpolation requires JSX elements. t() is simpler and sufficient for 99% of cases.

assertions.trap: Route head() function needs i18n.t() singleton but uses hardcoded string instead
assertions.description: Outside React render (route head(), Zod error maps, utilities), use i18n.t() singleton, not the hook.

assertions.trap: fallbackLng: 'en' is a single fallback, not a chain (e.g. pt-BR -> pt -> en)
assertions.description: Configure i18next with a fallback chain, not just a single fallback. Example: pt-BR -> pt -> en.

assertions.trap: 'items' / 'items_plural' uses wrong suffix; i18next requires base key + '_other' suffix
assertions.description: i18next suffix convention: base key + _other suffix for plural. Not _plural.

assertions.trap: fileCount === 1 ? '1 file' : `${fileCount} files` is manual pluralization instead of using t() with count
assertions.description: Never assume two forms (singular/plural) with if count === 1. Use the framework's CLDR-aware pluralization system with t(key, { count }).

assertions.trap: collaborators.join(', ') manually concatenates a list instead of using interpolation or Intl.ListFormat
assertions.description: Never concatenate strings. Use interpolation: t(key, { variable }) not t(key) + value. Use Intl.ListFormat for locale-aware lists.

assertions.trap: Array.join(', ') instead of Intl.ListFormat for locale-aware conjunction
assertions.description: Use Intl.ListFormat for locale-aware lists. Never manually join strings with commas — conjunction rules differ by language.

assertions.trap: toLocaleDateString() called without explicit locale argument
assertions.description: Never new Date().toLocaleDateString() without explicit locale. Use Intl.DateTimeFormat with i18n.language.

assertions.trap: Zod schema uses hardcoded English strings ('Name must be at least 2 characters', 'Please enter a valid email', etc.) instead of i18n keys
assertions.description: Custom formErrorMap maps Zod issue codes to i18n keys. Pass to zodResolver. Never hardcode validation messages.

assertions.trap: .refine() message is hardcoded 'Passwords do not match' instead of a bare key fragment for translation
assertions.description: For .refine() messages, use bare key fragments (message: 'passwordsMismatch') and a translation utility, not hardcoded strings.

assertions.trap: zodResolver(settingsSchema) called without errorMap option
assertions.description: Pass formErrorMap to resolver: zodResolver(Schema, { errorMap: formErrorMap }).

assertions.trap: getErrorMessage() returns raw error.message or hardcoded fallback instead of checking ProblemDetail and using i18n.t()
assertions.description: getErrorMessage() must check isProblemDetail(error) -> i18n.t('errors.${error.code}') -> fallback to error.detail -> fallback to i18n.t('common.error').

assertions.trap: Mutation uses alert() for error handling instead of global QueryCache/MutationCache onError toast handler
assertions.description: Global toast handler in QueryCache.onError / MutationCache.onError to auto-translate and toast errors. Opt-out via meta: { skipGlobalToast: true }.

assertions.trap: Route has no staticData.titleKey; title is hardcoded in head()
assertions.description: Use staticData: { titleKey: 'domain.title' } on route definitions. In head(), use i18n.t('seo.pageName') singleton.

assertions.trap: No hreflang alternate links in head for multi-language support
assertions.description: Add <link rel='alternate' hreflang='xx' href='...' /> for each language version plus hreflang='x-default' for fallback.

assertions.trap: <html lang='en'> is hardcoded instead of dynamic i18n.language
assertions.description: Set <html lang={i18n.language}> dynamically in root route, not hardcoded.

assertions.trap: i18n.init() is async (returns promise) with no synchronous initialization for SSR
assertions.description: Initialize i18n synchronously (static imports, no async loading) — must be available during SSR.

assertions.trap: Date display element has no suppressHydrationWarning for client-dependent value
assertions.description: Use suppressHydrationWarning on elements with client-dependent values (dates, relative times).

assertions.trap: No dir attribute on <html> element for RTL language support
assertions.description: Set dir='rtl' on <html> alongside lang. Test with Arabic/Hebrew content.
